title: Multi-omics characterization of childhood rhabdomyosarcoma
projectDescription: Rhabdomyosarcoma (RMS) presents a significant challenge in the field of oncology as a high-grade, aggressive soft tissue sarcoma that originates from skeletal (striated) muscle cells which do not fully differentiate. This condition is characterized by two major molecular and histopathological subtypes: 'alveolar' RMS (ARMS) and 'embryonic' RMS (ERMS), each driven by unique underlying mechanisms. The disease's inherent heterogeneity, complex genetic makeup, and limited therapeutic options highlight the need for innovative research approaches to understand its complexities. Through the use of transcriptomics, alongside an exploration of proteomics, researchers have identified dysregulated proteins common to both subtypes, offering insights into the pathophysiology and aggressiveness of RMS. Additionally, metabolomics sheds light on metabolic pathways linked to RMS perturbation. The integration of high-throughput omics ‘Multi-omics’ technologies thus provides unprecedented opportunities to dissect the molecular intricacies of cancer biology, offering avenues for enhanced understanding and targeted interventions in RMS.
sampleProcessingProtocol: Extract for the plasma samples, proteins will be extracted using 1 ml lysis solution (8 M urea, 500 mM Tris HCl, pH 8.5). The lysate will be precipitated with acetone and assayed using BCA method. Peptide mixture will be placed on the C18 and centrifuged at 150 g/ 3 min to trap peptides on the cartilage.  For translation (transfer-elution), C18 cartilage will be washed with 20 µl of 0.2% FA and transferred to a new collection tubes. Peptide mixture will be collected by rinsing the cartilage with 80% acetonitrile (10 µl x3 times) where the collected elute contains desalted peptides ready for injection.
dataProcessingProtocol: MS/MS files from the Orbitrap Fusion Lumos will be analyzed by Proteome discoverer software. MS/MS spectra will be searched against the human database. Briefly, protein sequences will be downloaded from UniProt. The statistical programming language R is used for statistical data analysis. To identify correlations between samples, Spearman correlation will be applied, and the R package q-value will be used for calculating false discovery rate adjusted p-values (q-values). Results will be filtered for a q-value < 0.05. The detected proteome changes in different subgroups of RMS will be correlated with patients’ clinical data.
